notes.betweenness: High value means the node lies on many shortest causal-transport paths; candidate bottleneck.
notes.eigenvector_centrality: High value means the node receives influence from influential nodes; hub dominance proxy.
notes.assortativity: Positive means similar-strength nodes couple; negative means hub-periphery coupling.
notes.modularity: Higher modularity suggests separated metastable basins in the TE graph.
notes.spectral_gap: Larger gap means faster Markov mixing on the TE network; smaller gap means long-lived modes.

notes.betweenness: High value means the node lies on many shortest causal-transport paths; candidate bottleneck.
notes.eigenvector_centrality: High value means the node receives influence from influential nodes; hub dominance proxy.
notes.assortativity: Positive means similar-strength nodes couple; negative means hub-periphery coupling.

notes.CCDF: Use P(S>=s) on log-log axes; it is less noisy than histogram tails.
notes.LLR: Positive log-likelihood ratio favors power-law over lognormal; negative favors lognormal.
notes.p_value: Approximate Vuong test p-value when scipy is available. Low p means the sign of LLR is more reliable.
notes.fractal_dimension_box: Box-counting dimension of each spacetime avalanche cluster. Needs large clusters for reliability.
notes.front_velocity: Max node-distance reached divided by event duration.
